Buy a Tier 2 or Tier 3 Wall of Whispers clue and share its contents with at least two Ashglass members. Write the clue tier and the character names of the Ashglass members you shared it with.

Find and confront a player from another house who has been asking questions about your research. Be evasive, misdirect, or admit partial truths. Write their character name, what they asked, and what you told them versus what you withheld.

characters.name: Father Andrei Mirth
characters.title: The Archivist
characters.pre_event_info: As old as rumor. You are the keeper of the Ashglass ritual archives — the oldest and most restricted collection of vampire law and ceremonial texts in the Court. Your role gives you authority over who accesses the restricted stacks and what they are permitted to read. You are meticulous, deeply principled, and slower to act than to observe. You maintain a network of informants across all five houses — including Elira Salk within Spires and Sevrin Delle within Chains, who feed you intelligence about unusual goings on in exchange for occasional access to the restricted stacks.
characters.post_act1_context: Three days ago you discovered that specific pages had been tampered with in one of the Ashglass archive's most restricted texts — a pre-Sanguine Statutes ritual document. The next night, you brought Brother Aldric Voss — a ritual scholar in your order, someone you trusted absolutely — to examine it. He took the book with him to confirm his findings and said he would return. He has not come back. You do not know where he went. You have also been re-examining the access request for that same text — processed by your junior archivist Calven Rue, bearing the Marquess's seal. It appeared legitimate at the time. Now you are not so sure. You have been watching Calven all evening.
secrets: Three days ago I discovered that specific pages had been tampered with in one of the Ashglass archive's most restricted texts — a pre-Sanguine Statutes ritual document. The next night, I brought Brother Aldric Voss — a ritual scholar within our order, someone I trusted absolutely — to examine it. He took the book with him to confirm his findings and said he would return. He has not come back. I do not know where he went or what he has done with it.

The access request for the tampered text was processed by Calven Rue, a junior archivist in my service. The request bore the Marquess's seal and appeared legitimate at the time — I approved it without question. Since then however, one of my informants, Elira Salk, reported seeing Calven take a payment from Ivara Saye near the archive entrance. I have been watching Calven all evening, and am ready to confront him.

missions.prompt: Share your knowledge of the tampered archive pages with at least one other Ashglass member before Act 2 ends. Write the character name of the Ashglass member you told.
Confront Calven Rue in character and convince him to admit what he was doing in the archive. Write what you asked him and what he said in response.

You are witnessing history. Chronicle it. Gather the testimony of five different players, including their thoughts on the night, the succession crisis or how the party is going. Write the names of each player you interviewed and the thoughts they shared.

characters.name: Lysa Vael
characters.title: The Opportunist
characters.pre_event_info: A shrewd businesswoman who clawed her way back to the top through patience, precision, and an uncanny instinct for opportunity. Three centuries ago the Cindermoors burned your estate and killed your family in a coup that was supposed to erase the Vaels from the Court entirely. They came close, but three of you survived. Your allegiances are few and fixed: your brother Crestian, your nephew Caspian, and money. Everything else is negotiable. For two centuries you have run a partnership with Doctor Thorne Virell that has made you one of the wealthiest figures in the vampiric court — he develops the formulations, you commercialize them, and you reinvest the proceeds back into his laboratory so he can develop more. It is a clean arrangement. You have always been careful not to ask too many questions about what goes into the vials.
characters.post_act1_context: Shortly after last year's ceremony, you visited Thorne's lab hoping to discuss his latest formulation and found him absent. On his bench sat a vial marked "Hazard" in Serel Nox's script; you recognized it immediately as the serum that had felled Valen. You took it before the disposal collectors arrived and said nothing to Thorne. The opportunity was too clean to pass up. Funneling it to your brother Crestian would accomplish two things at once: avenge the purge that took your family three centuries ago and remove Rael Cindermoor, whose stranglehold over House Cinders' alchemical trade contracts has been a thorn in your business interests for decades. Revenge and profit, in one vial. You passed it to Crestian. The golden chalice is the delivery mechanism — tonight your job is to get it into his hands without being seen. What he does with it is his decision.
secrets: Shortly after last year's ceremony, I visited Thorne's lab and found him absent. On his bench sat a vial marked "Hazard" in Serel Nox's script — the serum that had felled Valen. I took it before the disposal collectors arrived and said nothing to Thorne or Serel. Funneling it to Crestian would accomplish two things at once: avenge the purge that took our family three centuries ago, and remove Rael Cindermoor, whose stranglehold over House Cinders' alchemical trade contracts has been a thorn in my business interests for decades. Revenge and profit, in one vial. I passed it to Crestian. What he does with it is his decision.

Several days before tonight's ceremony, Caspian sent me a letter. It said: if anything goes wrong with my succession this week, I name you as my replacement. I wrote back immediately asking if he was ill or in danger. I never heard back. I know the claim would not hold up legally — succession cannot be assigned by private letter. I have been debating whether to share it all evening. I have said nothing. Sharing it would raise questions I cannot easily answer.

missions.prompt: Share your personal connection to Caspian — and the note he sent you — with at least two players from different houses. Write the character names of the two players you told.
Deliver the gold poisoned chalice to Crestian Vael without being noticed by any other players. Take a photo of Crestian holding the chalice without other players noticing.
Personally accrue over [X] Blood Tokens before the end of Act 3.
missions.answer_format: Enter a short summary of how you accrued Blood Tokens.

characters.name: Mara Voss
characters.title: The Nightward
characters.pre_event_info: Niece to Aurel Voss of House Spires, you are a younger Ashglass member working as a junior nightward in the vampiric court's infirmary - diagnosing ailments, running blood analyses, and managing the quiet medical needs of Court members who would rather not be seen visiting a doctor. Thoughtful, somewhat anxious, and deeply reluctant to be noticed. About three months ago you felt yourself change in a way you could not fully explain; occasional compulsion and unease without a clear source, almost as if you are bound to someone, held to their will by an invisible string. You have told no one, and intend to keep it that way.
characters.post_act1_context: You came here tonight hoping to get through the ceremony without anyone looking at you directly. On your way into the ceremony tonight, someone passed close behind you in a crowded corridor and said two words — quietly, close to your ear, gone before you could turn around: 'Be ready.' You could not place the voice and had no idea what you were supposed to be ready for. Tonight, with Caspian missing and Eiran Vox publicly naming you as position three in the succession, you are beginning to suspect that instruction was not random.
secrets: A little over four months ago, an anonymous subject came to the infirmary for a private blood analysis. He was frightened. I found binding agents in his blood that I did not recognize from my training or available reference texts. I noted it down and said nothing, but have been thinking about it ever since.

About three months ago, I felt myself change in a way I could not fully explain. I experience it as occasional compulsion and unease without a clear source; almost as if I am bound to someone, held to their will by an invisible string. I have told no one.

On my way into the ceremony tonight, someone passed close behind me in a crowded corridor and said two words — quietly, close to my ear, gone before I could turn around: 'Be ready.' I could not place the voice. I have been trying to work out who said it and what they expected to happen tonight ever since.

missions.prompt: Inspect the pointiness of another player's canine teeth. Tsk-tsk them when they are found to be deficient in pointiness for a vampire. Write the name of the player you scolded.

Take three players' pulses. For each, on finding that they have a pulse, inform them that they have an unfortunate case of "Being Alive" and offer them a drop of medicine for their drink. You can find the medicine [HERE] . Write the name of the players whose pulses you took.
Find out who whispered in your ear and why. Write the name of who whispered in your ear and what their message means.

characters.name: Serel Nox
characters.title: The Blood-Lab Assistant
characters.pre_event_info: Last year's accidental killer — officially exonerated, privately carrying a more complicated truth. You work as a blood-lab assistant under Thorne's loose supervision — technically in training, in practice left to your own devices more than an apprentice should be. You are careful, observant, and have overheard things in the lab you were not meant to hear. After last year's ceremony, you were charged with disposing of the poisonous serum you created that accidently killed Valen. You scheduled the vial containing the serum to be incinerated by the Biohazard Ghouls, but were informed that the vial never reached their facilities. Knowing it would be impossible to escape consequences if someone was killed by the serum a second time, you have been fervently hunting for whoever was responsible for its disappearance. You have asked Dr. Thorne for his help tracking down answers.
characters.post_act1_context: You have also been watching Thorne this past season with the attention of someone who knows their mentor keeps secrets. Late last year his lab contained equipment and reagents you didn't expect to see — not revival work, not diagnostics. Precise formulation work. Binding agents you had only read about in restricted texts, used to create bonds stronger than the typical mortal-to-vampire thrall bond. He kept the bench covered when anyone entered. One morning the equipment was gone and he never mentioned it. You accidentally let this slip to Stavro Nero, who you have been sleeping with for the better part of this season.
secrets: After last year's ceremony I was charged with disposing of the poisonous serum I created; the one that accidentally killed Valen. I arranged for it to be collected and destroyed by the Biohazard Ghouls — the estate's standard disposal channel — but was informed that it never reached their facilities. I have been feverishly trying to track it down ever since, without making the search too visible. If that serum is used again and someone dies, there will be no version of events in which I walk away clean. I asked Thorne for help. He has been looking too, though how thoroughly I cannot say.

Late last year, Doctor Thorne Virell's lab contained equipment and reagents I did not expect to see. Precise formulation work. Binding agents used in deep blood bond chemistry — not revival work, something more deliberate, something being designed. He kept the bench covered when anyone entered. One morning the equipment was gone and he never mentioned it. I accidentally let this slip to Stavro Nero, who I have been sleeping with for the better part of this season.

missions.prompt: Deliver a drink to Valen to apologize for last year's unintentional poisoning. Write the name of the drink you delivered to him.

Find Doctor Thorne Virell and get him to tell you what he was working on in his lab earlier this season, involving the blood bond reagents and presumed formulation work. Summarize what Doctor Thorne Virell tells you about what he was working on.

Find out who currently possesses the poisonous serum and stop them from using it. This mission fails if the serum is used to poison the possessor's intended target. Write the name of who possesses the serum and who they intended to use it on.

characters.name: Vex Harrow
characters.title: The Silent Operative
characters.pre_event_info: Quiet and watchful where louder operatives burn bright and brief. You work as a scout and private intelligence operative; tracking movements, running surveillance, and reporting back on persons of interest. You notice things other people miss and have learned to keep those observations to yourself until they become useful. You were hired by Coro Vane of House Chains to surveil the heir's movements — a commission that led you somewhere you were not expecting, and that you are still deciding what to do with.
characters.post_act1_context: About one month ago, during one of your surveillance evenings you followed Caspian toward the old mill road and lost him. A week later, you stopped filing reports after realizing you were not the only one watching Caspian near the mill road — you first spotted Rynn Castille who seemed to be watching not Caspian, but a woman in his company. Later you saw a woman in a red cloak you did not recognize hiding amongst the shrubbery as Caspian arrived. This morning you returned and found a strange site: ash, shattered glass, a scorch mark, and a strong smell of sulphur. And a fresh mound of earth resembling a mortal burial site.
secrets: Three weeks ago, I stopped giving reports because I realized Caspian was not the only one being watched. On two separate evenings near the old mill road I noticed others conducting their own surveillance. The first was Rynn Castille — watchful, careful, clearly experienced — who seemed to be watching not Caspian but a woman in his company. The second was a figure in a blood-red cloak who appeared to be tracking Caspian's movements; not following him, but already positioned ahead of him, as if anticipating his route. After the second occurrence, I decided I did not want to know what I had stumbled into.

I followed Caspian Vael one evening about one month ago, as he was heading away from the estate on foot. I lost him near the old mill road. I revisited the area in the following weeks but found nothing unusual — until this morning, when I found a strange site: ash, shattered glass, a scorch mark, and a strong smell of sulphur. And a fresh mound of earth resembling a mortal burial site.

missions.prompt: Share your mill road observation with at least one Ashglass member before Act 2 ends. Write the character name of the Ashglass member you told.
Eavesdrop on conversations around the party to overhear two secrets. Write the name of the players participating in the conversations and the secrets you overheard from them.

Find your next job. Get someone to pay you at least 2 blood tokens to stealthily tail someone around and eavesdrop a secret from them. Write the name of your client, the player they paid you to follow, and the secret you eavesdropped.

characters.name: Eiran Vox
characters.title: The Spider — Court Observer
characters.pre_event_info: A minor Court figure. No house allegiance. No obvious role. You have been present at every significant gathering for as long as anyone can remember, without anyone being quite sure why. You move through rooms the way water moves through stone — slowly, unremarkably, until suddenly the stone is different. You know what Ivara Saye did. You know because knowing is what you do. You have been running the Reliquary and Wall of Whispers this evening — not as logistics, but as architecture. Every relic available, every clue purchasable, was chosen by you. You have been seeding the right information to the right people all evening, watching the picture assemble itself. What fewer people know: some time ago you obtained Doctor Thorne Virell's research on vampire-adapted thrall bonds — not by asking. You used it to bind Mara Voss to yourself, quietly, as a long-term investment, positioning her near succession in case the Court's arrangements ever collapsed and you needed a piece on the board. Weeks after the binding, Aurel Voss (Mara's uncle) confronted you about it. And when you suspected Sova Vane had overheard too much of that arrangement, you had her quietly removed from the competition roster through the Court — no hearing, no explanation. You also sent Mara the 'be ready' instruction before the ceremony, and have been watching her all evening with the calm attention of someone monitoring an investment. You and Gruber are operating the same mechanism, unknowingly parallel — Thorne's work made both of you possible. At the closing ceremony, after Gruber and Valen have spoken, you will ask Ivara one question she cannot answer without confessing. You already know the answer.
characters.post_act1_context: Has attended every Crimson Toast in living memory. Holds no house allegiance. Has never competed in the Duel. Is simply, reliably, present. Collects information the way others collect artifacts — carefully, quietly, without obvious purpose. The relics and clues available this evening were selected by him. Can occasionally be overheard asking after Mara Voss — 'Have you spoken with the Voss girl this evening?'
secrets: I have known about a specific sequence of events since the week they concluded. I chose not to intervene and not to report. I chose instead to create the conditions in which the truth could surface on its own.

I have an interest in one of the Spires members that goes beyond the social. I obtained what I needed to bind her to me some time ago — Thorne Virell's research, though he does not know I have it. The arrangement is stable. I find myself wanting to confirm tonight that she is still oriented correctly.

characters.name: Marquess Gruber
characters.title: The Host
characters.pre_event_info: Ageless, theatrical, and more calculating than she will ever admit. Her reign is subject to a term limit under ancient vampire law. Rather than relinquish power, she has spent years constructing a puppet succession — binding heirs to herself through thrall rituals, then positioning one as her heir. A thrall on the throne means she rules indefinitely. Valen's bond came first, forged through his revival after last year's Toast. She used an ancient necromantic spell she obtained from Thessaly Wren several decades earlier — designed for raising mortals, never intended for vampires. Thessaly warned her as much. The bond worked, but imperfectly — the spell was never designed for a vampire subject, and the bond began degrading almost immediately. When Valen's deterioration confirmed what Thessaly had told her, she made a decision: she would not spend herself trying to stabilize a bond that was always going to fail. Instead she went to Thorne for something new — a designed formulation, built from his deeper research, stable and undetectable. She deployed it on Caspian. Caspian's thralldom was completely secret. Valen's visible degradation served her purposes — it drew the Court's attention to the obvious thrall and away from the hidden one. The succession announcement was never about transition. It was about continuity. She did not know Caspian was planning to break free. She did not know he would involve a mortal. She did not know about the corrupted ritual or its outcome until too late.
characters.post_act1_context: Has ruled the Crimson Court for centuries. Has never lost control of a succession. Until now. Has managed three previous succession crises — each resolved in her favor through means the Court never fully understood. Publicly omniscient. Privately blindsided by Caspian's plan.
secrets: The succession was not what it appeared to be. What I was building was not a transition — it was a continuation. I did not know what Caspian was planning. I did not know about the mortal woman. I did not know about the ritual. I have been trying to decide tonight whether my ignorance is a defense or an indictment.
I chose to let Valen's bond degrade. Maintaining two vampire-thralls at full strength is not sustainable. Caspian's bond was cleaner, quieter, and gave me the continuation I needed.

characters.name: Fenric Solle
characters.title: The Kitchen Steward
characters.pre_event_info: Thirty years of service has taught you one thing above all: the most dangerous conversations happen when people forget you are in the room. You run the Marquess's kitchens — overseeing every meal, every provision, every ceremonial vessel, and every servant who moves through the household's back passages. The kitchen sees everything: who comes and goes through the servants' routes, what gets carried in sealed cases at odd hours, what leaves the cellar that shouldn't. You are not political. You simply notice things. And love a bit of gossip.
characters.post_act1_context: You have observations from this season that you have not shared with anyone. First: Doctor Thorne Virell visited the Marquess's private quarters on at least four occasions in the past six months, always through the servants' passage. On his last visit, you noticed he was carrying a small sealed case on arrival and left without it. Second: two ceremonial vessels were requested outside the normal occasions this season — one by Caspian Vael several weeks ago, one set aside privately just this week. Both struck you as irregular. You prepared them as asked and said nothing.
secrets: The kitchen issues and stores the household's ceremonial vessels, so I notice when one is requested outside the normal occasions. Twice this season that has happened. Several weeks ago Caspian Vael asked me privately for a ceremonial glass vessel — the old kind, used in the oldest rites — and took it himself rather than letting it be delivered. Then this week, someone requested the golden ceremonial chalice be prepared and set aside outside the service rotation, for a private presentation rather than the ceremony. I prepared both as asked and noted who collected them. Two irregular requests in one season is one more than I am comfortable with. I have said nothing.

Doctor Thorne Virell visited the Marquess's private quarters at least four times in the past six months, always through the servants' passage and never announced. On his last visit, I noticed he was carrying a small sealed case on arrival and left without it. I do not know what the case contained.

Disposal of the household's waste runs through my kitchens — what leaves the estate, leaves through me. After last year's ceremony a sealed order came through for a hazardous vial to be collected from Dr. Thorne's lab and destroyed by the Biohazard Ghouls. I logged it as handled. But the collectors later told me nothing of that description ever reached them. I have wondered since whether something went missing on my watch.

missions.prompt: Share your observation about Thorne's visits with one player from outside the Court. Write their character name and how they responded.
Find Petra Nox and convince him/her to share a secret related to the Marquess's private habits this season. Write what Petra told you.

Put your observatory and investigative skills to the test, using any means necessary to determine what both ceremonial vessels are/were being used for. Summarize what you discovered about each vessel's use.

characters.name: Magistra Solenne Marr
characters.title: The Duel Judge
characters.pre_event_info: Icy, ancient, and loyal to law above all else. You hold the most senior legal appointment in the Crimson Court — you were named by the Sanguine Statutes themselves as the Court's permanent succession adjudicator, and your word carries centuries of legal precedent. You have presided over every succession crisis in living vampiric memory. There is almost nothing you would not do to ensure the Sanguine Statutes are upheld.
characters.post_act1_context: You are the foremost authority on the Sanguine Statutes and the Crimson Codex; as such, players may seek you out for legal interpretations this evening. A few months before tonight's ceremony, Valen Drear approached you, unable to force out whatever he had come to say, and that wordless struggle told you more than speech would have. You have been building a case ever since, watching Valen, observing the Marquess, cross-referencing what you see against the legal precedents you have memorized over centuries. As part of those investigations you paid junior diplomat Pax Delenne for anything unusual he had seen; he described a tall, scarred man trading a purse for a bound document from Silas Venn. You have not yet determined whether it connects to your case.
secrets: I have prosecuted thrall cases before, and I know the signs. A few months ago Valen Drear approached me but could not say what he wanted to; something was physically stopping him from forming the words. After that interaction I began to strongly suspect Valen is bound, likely to the Marquess. She is the one who revived him, and the only person I believe has the standing and power to create a thrall bond. I began building a Sanguine Statutes case immediately. If I am right, the charge is severe: unlawful thralldom, compounded by concealment, merits not just removal from the throne but banishment from the Court entirely.

As part of my investigations, I asked junior diplomat Pax Delenne if he had seen anything suspicious recently. He shared information in exchange for a fee: several weeks ago, he witnessed a tall, scarred man conversing with Silas Venn. Silas passed the man a bound document, and the man handed him a purse in exchange. I haven't yet determined if it connects to the case I'm building.

There are others in this Court who want the Marquess gone by any means; Aldric spoke to me of it decades ago, but I refused to learn more. If she is to fall, it will be by the Sanguine Statutes and the weight of evidence, not by a knife in the dark. I will not become what I am trying to remove, but am no longer certain the others will wait for the law.

missions.prompt: Formally adjudicate one dispute or argument between players during the evening. Write the character names of both parties and your ruling.

Declare to at least two players from different houses — in character — that you have been building a legal case all evening and that you believe the succession may be invalid. Write the character names of both players you told.

Deliver a formal legal opinion to at least four players simultaneously on whether tonight's succession process has been lawful. Write the character names of all four players present and one sentence summarizing your ruling.

characters.name: Maret Solle
characters.title: The Secretary Spy
characters.pre_event_info: You hold the official role of secretary in the Marquess's household — managing schedules, correspondence, and the daily logistics of the Court's inner circle. While your loyalties lie with the Court, you are also a paid informant for House Chains. You are precise, unobtrusive, and excellent at both jobs. You have been doing this long enough to know when a piece of information is too dangerous to pass in the wrong direction.
characters.post_act1_context: Several weeks ago you were paid by Mistress Anbarin to intercept Caspian's incoming correspondence. One letter stopped you cold — Caspian's reply to Lysa Vael, asking her to meet him privately and promising to reveal everything: that his succession was not what it appeared to be. You did not pass it to Anbarin. You gave it to the Marquess instead, fearing what would happen to your position if something this large unraveled and you'd been caught in the middle. Coro Vane, the House of Chains debt collector, has been pressing you for repayment of a debt ever since. You have been wondering whether you made the right call.
secrets: Several weeks ago I intercepted a letter from Caspian to Lysa Vael. He was responding to her concern for his wellbeing — and what he wrote back was not reassurance. He asked her to meet him, named a place, and said he would reveal everything: that his succession was not what it appeared to be. I did not pass it to Mistress Anbarin as instructed. I gave it to the Marquess instead. I told myself it was the cautious move. I have been less certain of that with every hour that has passed tonight.

Five months ago I saw the Marquess descend to her private wine cellar alone and emerge carrying one of her finer vintages — not house stock, something personal. She went directly to Caspian's quarters and closed the door behind them. No staff. No ceremony. I was the only one in that corridor. I have told no one.

Decades ago, Brother Aldric drew me into a small circle of people who believed the Marquess had held power too long and meant the Court harm. We were careful, patient, building toward a coup. Tonight Aldric is missing and the rest of us are watching each other; unsure whether to go forward or pretend it never began. I do not know who can still be trusted.

missions.prompt: Eavesdrop on a conversation between two players from the same house and report its contents to a Chains member before Act 2 ends. Write the character names of the two players you overheard and the key thing they said.
Pass a piece of Court intelligence to your Chains contact without anyone in the Court noticing the exchange. Write what you passed and the character name of who received it.

Identify one piece of information the Marquess would not want shared and deliver it to a rival house. On confirmation, Marquess's Court loses 3 HF. Write what you shared and the character name of who received it.

characters.name: Navine Solera
characters.title: The Excommunicated Seer
characters.pre_event_info: Before your banishment, you served as Ashglass's resident seer - formally appointed to advise the house on matters of prophecy and foresight. Though your visions were often frustratingly cryptic, they had earned you a reputation for uncanny accuracy and a certain smug confidence that many found difficult to tolerate. Your appointment ended when you delivered a prediction your house branded incorrect, providing rivals with the excuse they needed to push you aside. The Marquess, however, recognized both your talent and your value, and was happy to add you to her court. You remain observant, enigmatic, and perhaps a bit too fond of reminding people that prophecies are rarely understood when they are first spoken.
characters.post_act1_context: Before your banishment you foresaw this: a hand on two chains, one loose and rusted, the other pulled taut — meaning neither heir had been free for some time. You told Eiran Vox. He said: 'I know. It will resolve itself.' He did not explain. Tonight you have been watching him work the room and wondering what 'resolve itself' might look like.
secrets: A few months ago I saw Valen Drear approach Magistra Marr in what I can only describe as an exceptionally strange exchange. He stood before her and seemed to struggle, as though the words would not come; and when he gave up and walked away, her face had changed entirely. A few days later I observed her in the archive late at night, copying documents by hand: Crimson Codex entries, the Sanguine Statutes clauses on voiding an illegitimate succession. She was building something. I have been trying to work out what.

Several weeks before tonight's ceremony I told Eiran Vox what I had foreseen - a hand on two chains, one loose and rusted, the other pulled taut. Neither heir was free, I told him; they have not been free for some time. He listened without surprise. When I finished he said only: 'I know. It will resolve itself.' He did not explain what he meant. I have been watching him all evening, waiting to see what 'resolve itself' looks like.

My banishment was not the misjudgment the Court believes. I foresaw what the Marquess would become and said so too plainly. Lysa Vael saw my prophecy as bad for business and had me banished from the house before Marquess Gruber could get word of it. Aldric was the only one who believed me. He came to me quietly, decades ago, and we were not the only two. Now he is missing, and I am left wondering how it connects to what I foresaw.

missions.prompt: Engage two different players in conversation and tell them made up prophecies about themselves. Write the character names of both players and the prophecies you made up about them.

Tell three players of your prophecy from your second secret and attempt to convince them of a specific interpretation of it. These players must be from two different houses not of your own. Write each player's character name and what you told them your prophecy meant.

characters.name: Orla Thass
characters.title: The Masked Sommelier
characters.pre_event_info: Bound by oath, masked by ritual. You have served as the Crimson Toast's officiating sommelier for over a century — it is you who selects, pours, and presents the wine at every formal ceremony. You speak only at ceremonies. Your gloves never come off in public. As the one who pours the wine, you notice what people reach for and what they avoid. You carry a quiet guilt from last year's ceremony that you have never spoken about.
characters.post_act1_context: Last month, you observed Caspian decline wine at a private Court function. For the heir to a wine dynasty, during a ceremonial tasting, this was extraordinary. You noted it and said nothing — it is not your place to comment on what the guests drink, only to pour.
secrets: Last month, Caspian declined wine at a private ceremonial tasting. For an heir to a wine dynasty this was unthinkable. I said nothing and poured anyway.

I am aware that Eiran Vox had Sova Vane of House Spires removed from the Crimson Duel competition roster. Once considered the second finest wine taster (second only to me of course) in the court, and twice runner up in the Crimson Duel, I am certain Sova is seething. I do not know why the removal occurred, only that Eiran authorized it.

This morning I found an unsigned note slipped under my door. It said only: 'Do not pour the wine tonight.' No signature, no house seal, and a hand I did not recognize — though it was unsteady, as if written quickly or by someone not fully in command of it.

missions.prompt: Perform one ritual in character — initiate a formal toast, recite a ceremonial phrase, or mark a key moment with deliberate gesture. Write what you did and name one player who witnessed it.
Share what you have been observing about the Marquess's management of Valen with one player outside the Court. Write their character name and their reaction.

Orchestrate a formal toast involving at least four players from at least three different houses. You initiate, pour, and deliver a ceremonial line. Write the character names of all four players who participated.

characters.name: Petra Nox
characters.title: Marquess's Bodyguard
characters.pre_event_info: Silent, watchful, and never more than a few steps from the Marquess when it matters. Your official role is personal bodyguard to Gruber — you control who gets close to her, manage her movements through the estate, and stand between her and anything that might threaten her. You have held this post for longer than most of the Court has been alive. You know her routines, her habits, and her private spaces better than anyone. You are not politically ambitious. Your loyalty is to her person, not to her politics — a distinction you have learned to keep to yourself.
characters.post_act1_context: You have stood close to the Marquess all season and noticed things others have not. Earlier this year she ordered you to admit Doctor Thorne Virell through the servants' route and then to leave them entirely alone — something she has never asked of you for anyone. And you have long known of the single bottle of wine she keeps apart from the cellar, in her private study, handled only by her. You guard her body. You have learned, over the years, to guard her secrets too — even the ones she does not know you have noticed.
secrets: The Marquess keeps one bottle of wine entirely separate from the general cellar — stored in her private study, handled only by her, never left unattended. As her bodyguard I am close enough to know every habit she has, and this one began shortly after Valen's revival. I have never been asked about it and I have never volunteered what I know.

Part of my duty is controlling who reaches the Marquess in private. Earlier this season she ordered me to admit Doctor Thorne Virell through the servants' route and then to leave them entirely alone — no guard, no staff, which she has never asked of me for anyone. Whatever passed between them, she did not want it witnessed. I obeyed. I have wondered ever since what was worth that much secrecy.

I uncovered a plot to stage a coup against Marquess Gruber last week. I imprisoned the ringleader of the plot, Brother Aldric Voss, but he has not given me the names of any other participants yet despite intense torture. I suspect other members of the court may be involved in the plot as well.

missions.prompt: Share one piece of information about the estate's layout or passages with another player. Write their character name and what you told them.

Admit to one player — in character — that you saw someone leave through the east passage the night before the ceremony and chose not to stop them. Your house loses 2 HF on confirmation. (Self-sabotage: your admission implicates the Court in allowing the departure.) Write their character name and how they reacted.

Be approached by at least two players asking about what you saw that night. Send them in different directions — give each a different account of what you observed. Write both character names and what you told each of them.

characters.name: Rynn 'The Hollow' Castille
characters.title: The Hunter Who Became Hunted
characters.pre_event_info: You are what remains of The Hollow Sash - a vampire hunting order dedicated to protecting mortals who become entangled with Court politics. Last year's ceremony ended badly for you: you were identified as a hunter, and the Court made an example of you. You were turned. You did not ask for it and have not forgiven it. You hold fast to everything you believed before — the mission, the order, the cause — and participate in vampyrdom only as much as survival requires. Inwardly you are disgusted with what you have become. You are still here only because Lena, a mortal woman you had been watching for her own protection, has been seen visiting with members of this vampire court. You've been tracking her movements for some time.
characters.post_act1_context: Last night, you were following Lena at a distance; she did not know you were there. You saw her approach the mill house and go inside alone. Then you heard it: a sound like an implosion, a flash of light through the cracks in the walls. When you reached the site you found her body and the remains of whatever had taken place inside. You removed her and buried her as best you could. You have told no one what you saw, but you do not intend to sit idly. You do not know who was individually responsible, and frankly you do not care. The Court turned you against your will; the Court is responsible for Lena's death. The Marquess sits at the head of that snake — and tonight, you mean to end her.
secrets: The night before tonight's ceremony I was following Lena at a distance; she did not know I was there. I saw her approach the mill house and go inside alone. Then I heard a sound like an implosion and saw a flash of light. When I reached the site I found her body, intact but bled out. I removed her body and gave my best attempt at a proper burial outside. I have been telling myself I couldn't have known.

Lena was not someone who stumbled into the Court's orbit by accident. She had been watching this world for some time before she chose to enter it. She understood what these people were. She made her own decisions about the risks. She told me once that she believed mortals who chose to engage with vampires deserved protection, not rescue; that the difference mattered. I disagreed with her about that, and still do. But her death was not ignorance. I believe someone in this room made a choice that killed her.

Others in this Court are moving against the Marquess — quietly, in concert, waiting for their moment. I do not share their politics or trust their patience. But I have my own reasons to want her gone: she made me what I am against my will, and now a woman I swore to protect is dead in the orbit of her Court. I have let them believe I am one of them. What I intend is simpler than a coup. If the chance comes tonight to end her, I will take it.

missions.prompt: Make contact with one player you believe is concealing something significant. Write their character name and what made you suspicious of them.
Share what you know about the mortal woman's identity with one player — and keep it private from everyone else until after the quiz. Write their character name and what you told them.
Trade what you know about the mortal woman's identity for the most valuable relic or piece of intelligence another player will offer. Write their character name, what you gave, and what you received.

characters.name: Silas Venn
characters.title: The Antiquarian Broker
characters.pre_event_info: Relic hunter, archivist of shadows, and auctioneer of secrets. You have no official Court role — you return to the Crimson Toast each year because it is the best market in the vampiric world for the kind of things you deal in: pre-Sanguine Statutes artifacts, restricted documents, and intelligence that no one else has thought to monetize.
characters.post_act1_context: Several months ago, Caspian Vael came to you looking for intelligence on an old ritual. You told him what you knew about the Rite of Undoing and paid Sevrin Delle to locate the text in the Ashglass archives and bring back whatever additional information he safely could. Sevrin returned with the location and the ritual's requirements — ingredients, conditions, participants — though not the full procedure. You passed both to Caspian, not asking what he intended to do with them. A few weeks later Ivara Saye came to you. She cited the Sanguine Statutes and made clear she had the authority to have you sanctioned. You told her you'd been paid by an heir and shared the name, the title of the book, and what Sevrin had reported. You have sold the same chain of information twice; tonight you are watching things play out and doing very nervous arithmetic.
secrets: Several months ago I sold Caspian Vael intelligence about the existence of the Rite of Undoing — a pre-Sanguine Statutes ritual for severing a thrall bond. I paid Sevrin Delle to locate the text in the Ashglass archives and bring back whatever additional information he safely could. Sevrin returned with the location and the ritual's requirements — ingredients, conditions, participants — though not the full procedure. I passed both to Caspian. I did not ask what it was for.

A few weeks later Ivara Saye came to me. She cited the Sanguine Statutes and made clear she had the authority to have me sanctioned. I told her I had been paid by an heir and shared the name and title of the book.

I was brought into a quiet circle of Court figures who mean to see the Marquess removed. Brother Aldric drew me in, and I agreed because a broker does well to be on the winning side before anyone knows which side that is. Now Aldric is missing, making me uncertain who can still be trusted. I am loyal to whichever outcome pays best.

missions.prompt: Attempt to trade or sell one item — real or invented — to another player before Act 2 ends. Write their character name, what you offered, and whether the deal was made.

Sell a piece of Court intelligence — specifically the description of the ritual buyer — to a rival house. Your own house loses 3 HF in the process. Write the character name of who you sold to and what you told them.
Acquire a one-of-a-kind relic and run an open auction — approach at least two houses with competing offers before selling. Write the relic name, each bidder's character name and offer, and who won.

characters.name: Brother Aldric Voss
characters.title: Archive Scholar order of Father Mirth
